name: Tambov Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage, the bountiful soil and the bravery of the people of Tambov
loyalty, fidelity and the rivers of the region

description: A horizontal triband of red, white and blue with a narrow white centre stripe between two broad red and blue stripes; the regional coat of arms is placed near the hoist.
history: The flag of Tambov Oblast was adopted on 27 November 2003. The colours and arms recall the historic Tambov Governorate, whose seal showed a beehive symbolising the industriousness of the region's inhabitants.

features.symbolism: the red and blue stripes are taken from the historical Tambov provincial arms; the narrow white stripe between them represents purity and unites the two.
the regional coat of arms (a beehive with three bees on a blue field) placed near the hoist.
